productive_behavior: The controller profiled the immutable input before planning and exposed only bounded schema metadata and complete candidate role labels.
Gemma caught an incorrect Hedges J denominator in the first plan, and deterministic lint continued to reject unsafe semantic arm mapping.
The workflow failed closed before computation when four bounded plan repairs did not produce an evidence-ready protocol.
release_blockers: Each repair request contained only the latest audit, so earlier corrected requirements were not durable across later rounds.
A concrete nonblocking request to lock the site variable's role and raster dimensions was not carried into later repairs while another blocking repair kept the loop active.
The final plan again left the site role unresolved and failed deterministic semantic-arm mapping lint despite intermediate plans addressing those issues.
implemented_repairs: The controller now accumulates distinct blocking and nonblocking findings across an already-active bounded plan-repair cycle.
Every later repair receives the cumulative finding set and records it in plan repair history for auditability.

The repair contract requires preservation of satisfied historical requirements, resolution of operationally correctable comments, explicit omission of incidental variables without an established role, and concrete publication-ready raster specifications.
disposition: Rejected as a release acceptance run even though it failed closed correctly; retained as the regression fixture for monotonic plan-repair convergence.
